skill.name: ghe-transition
skill.description: Guides GitHub Elements phase transitions by validating gates, closing the current thread, opening the next thread, and syncing progress state.
skill.summary: Move GitHub Elements work between DEV, TEST, REVIEW, and merge phases with gate checks.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from markdown text and workflow terminology in SKILL.md. One filesystem concern remains because the skill permits writes to ~/.claude, a persistent hidden configuration directory, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Configuration Directory Write Permission
medium_findings.description: The skill states that a background agent may write to ~/.claude for plugin or settings fixes. This is a hidden home-directory configuration path, and writes there can persistently alter assistant or plugin behavior beyond the current project. The instruction is framed as a boundary, not active malware, so this is a controlled but material risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 26 explicitly names ~/.claude as a writable location. The broader context limits writes, but the target is persistent configuration storage, so the risk is credible.

medium_findings.title: Overbroad Agent Control Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill uses absolute language that tells the agent to stop and wait for user guidance when a specification issue is found. This can interfere with normal autonomous workflow behavior, but it does not tell the agent to ignore security policies or bypass review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is strongly authoritative, but it focuses on preserving user specifications. I did not find evidence that it impersonates system instructions or asks the agent to skip safety analysis.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown inline-code text as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited lines define report naming, settings keys, and a related skill name. No executable shell command or dynamic command construction appears in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown documentation enclosed in inline-code formatting. There is no script block, command invocation, or interpolation behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md lines 3, 39, and 44. The surrounding text describes workflow transitions, report naming, and a requirements directory. No cryptographic operation or hash algorithm usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and markdown placeholders only. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance hits point to workflow headings and validation text about target phases. They do not instruct the agent to inventory the host, enumerate secrets, or collect system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited terms are part of a GitHub Elements phase-transition checklist. No host reconnaissance commands or data collection behavior appears in those lines.

content.value_statement: GitHub Elements workflows need controlled movement between DEV, TEST, REVIEW, and merge phases. This skill guides gate checks, thread closure, next-thread creation, and memory sync.
actual_capabilities: Defines valid DEV, TEST, REVIEW, and merge transitions.
Requires a phase-gate agent check before transition work continues.
Routes thread closure to the correct phase-specific thread manager.
Specifies completion comments, thread closure, next-thread creation, and thread linking.
Directs memory sync updates after a successful phase change.
Distinguishes phase transitions from progress checkpoints.
limitations: It depends on separate phase-gate and thread-manager agents.
It does not provide implementation code for GitHub API operations.
It is specific to the GitHub Elements workflow model.
It allows configuration writes to ~/.claude, which needs review before use.

use_cases.title: Move completed development into testing
use_cases.description: Use the skill after DEV work is complete and tests are ready for validation.

use_cases.title: Promote passing test work to review
use_cases.description: Use the skill when TEST has passed and the work needs a review thread.

use_cases.title: Demote failed review work back to development
use_cases.description: Use the skill when REVIEW fails and the issue needs a DEV thread for correction.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic transition
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a normal phase change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-transition to move this issue from DEV to TEST after checking the required gate.

prompt_templates.title: Move tested work to review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-transition to promote this issue from TEST to REVIEW. Confirm the phase gate result before changing threads.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a review failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a demotion path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-transition to demote this issue from REVIEW to DEV because the review verdict is FAIL. Preserve links between threads.

prompt_templates.title: Complete review and prepare merge
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for the final approved transition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-transition for a REVIEW to merge transition after a PASS verdict. Include the validation result and memory sync status.

output_examples.input: Move the current DEV issue to TEST.
output_examples.output: Phase gate passed. The DEV thread was closed, the TEST thread was opened, links were added, and memory was updated.

output_examples.input: Promote TEST to REVIEW if all checks pass.
output_examples.output: Validation completed. All tests passed, so the TEST thread was closed and a REVIEW thread was created with links.

output_examples.input: Review failed. Send the work back to DEV.
output_examples.output: Review failed. The REVIEW thread was closed, a DEV correction thread was opened, and progress state was synchronized.

best_practices: Use this skill only when the phase should change.
Confirm completion comments and gate criteria before closing a thread.
Review any ~/.claude write request before allowing persistent configuration changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill only to save progress.
Do not skip the phase-gate check before transition work.
Do not use invalid direct transitions such as DEV to REVIEW.

faq.question: What workflow does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports GitHub Elements phase movement across DEV, TEST, REVIEW, merge, and documented demotion paths.

faq.question: Can it save progress without changing phase?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to use ghe-checkpoint when progress should be saved without a phase change.

faq.question: Does it run GitHub commands by itself?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill describes agent responsibilities and workflow steps, not executable GitHub commands.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill permits writes to ~/.claude, which can persistently affect local assistant or plugin configuration.

faq.question: Which transitions are blocked?
faq.answer: The skill blocks DEV to REVIEW and REVIEW to TEST because they bypass required workflow paths.

faq.question: Which AI tools are listed as supported?
faq.answer: The generated metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
